title: Anton Import Format
description: Canonical JSON shape for Anton's metadata.json import contract. v0.3 — adds optional round-trip fidelity fields so an Anton-native export can be re-imported losslessly (anton#231, rdf-bundle-reimport): `formset_id`, `term_values` and `_raw` on entries, `notes` on inline authority specs (actor/place/keyword biographies), and media identity (`collection_name`, `file_name`, `generated_conversions`, `av_duration_seconds`) on File. All additive and optional — pre-v0.3 documents (and external producers such as agate) continue to validate unchanged. v0.2 added optional `nara_category`; v0.2.1 added optional `Note.tracks`.

version.description: Schema major.minor that this document targets (e.g. "0.1").

tenant.description: Anton tenant slug for which the import is intended (e.g. "kba", "gosteli"). Optional and nullable: producer-side pipelines (notably agate) often don't know the target tenant; the consumer-side importer overrides this with its own setting('slug') anyway.

generator.description: Identifier of the producing tool, including version. Convention: "<package>@<version>" (e.g. "agate@0.1.0", "anton-excel-import@0.58.2").

defaults.description: Pipeline-wide defaults inherited by authority specs that don't set match_by/on_not_found.

entries.description: Top-level Anton-import records (collection / record entries forming the hierarchy).

MatchBy.description: Strategy for matching an inline authority spec against an existing DB authority record.

OnNotFound.description: Policy applied when the inline authority spec does not match any existing DB record.

formset_id.description: Which ObjectForm/Objectformtype produced the record — governs which fields exist. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

_raw.description: Opaque snapshot of exact pre-transform column values for byte-fidelity restore. Consumer-defined; non-Anton producers SHOULD omit. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

formset_id.description: Which ObjectForm/Objectformtype produced the record — governs which fields exist. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

_raw.description: Opaque snapshot of exact pre-transform column values for byte-fidelity restore. Consumer-defined; non-Anton producers SHOULD omit. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

TermValue.description: A termselect value: a controlled term chosen for a specific antonfield (e.g. Reproduktionsbestimmungen). `antonfield` names the field; the term is referenced by `term_id` (same-tenant) and/or `taxonomy` + `name` (portable). Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

NaraCategory.description: NARA-standard file format category. Producers emit the raw NARA category; the consumer (Anton) maps it to its tenant `object_types` taxonomy. The 16 categories cover NARA's File Format Risk Assessment vocabulary as of 2024.

ObjectReference.description: Reference to an existing AntonObject (parent, etc.). UUID is primary; identifier and id only resolve if the target already exists in DB. Resolution order: uuid > identifier > id.

MultilingualString.description: Multilingual content keyed by ISO-639-1 two-letter language codes (de, fr, en, it, ...). Values are the localised strings.

LanguageCode.description: ISO-639-2 three-letter language code as stored in Anton's languages.name column (ger, fre, eng, lat, ...).

AuthorityReference.description: Reference to an Actor, Place, or Keyword. Either id-form (existing DB record by id) or inline-form (match-or-create). The two forms MUST NOT be mixed.

notes.description: Notes on the authority record itself (e.g. actor biography / bioghist). Reuses the object Note shape. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

type.description: Eventtype name (matches Anton's Eventtype enum: creation, acquisition, reproduction, digitisation, reception, performance, provenance, preservation, engravation, writing, coloring, edition, production, text_author, ingest, other).
Notetype name (e.g. scopecontent, archivists_notes, general_note, internal_note, condition_of_access, provenance, ...).

locale.description: ISO-639-1 two-letter locale code for the note text.

tracks.description: Optional time-stamped track entries — used by movie_content / audio_content notes that carry a per-timestamp transcript or scene description. Producers MAY omit; consumers MUST treat as additive (pre-tracks Notes remain valid).

timestamp.description: Timecode within the parent media — typically HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.mmm. Format is producer-defined; consumers SHOULD treat as opaque text.

content.description: Free-text track content at this timestamp (transcript line, scene description, OCR snippet, …).

collection_name.description: Anton media collection (image / audio / video / application). Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

file_name.description: Stored original filename (may differ from display `name`). Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

generated_conversions.description: Per-derivative conversion state (e.g. { "web": { "width": 800, "height": 600 }, "thumb": true }), so the consumer can restore or re-drive conversions. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).

av_duration_seconds.description: Audio/video duration in seconds. Round-trip fidelity (v0.3).
